input.instructions: 1) Core text: The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic. 2) Entities and roles: The investigator (subject/actor) and the witness (object/potential subject). 3) Linguistic phenomenon: Pronoun disambiguation and ambiguity detection regarding the pronouns he and his. 4) Specific question: Identify which option correctly explains the antecedent of the pronouns in the context of the provided narrative. The task is to determine if the pronouns refer to the investigator or the witness based on semantic and syntactic cues.

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

Question:
1) Core text: The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic. 2) Entities and roles: The investigator (subject/actor) and the witness (object/potential subject). 3) Linguistic phenomenon: Pronoun disambiguation and ambiguity detection regarding the pronouns he and his. 4) Specific question: Identify which option correctly explains the antecedent of the pronouns in the context of the provided narrative. The task is to determine if the pronouns refer to the investigator or the witness based on semantic and syntactic cues.

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Both pronouns refer to the investigator
JUSTIFICATION: Contextual logic links investigator to being late and stuck.
